title: 26t1w09 worktree cleanup
description:
Cleanup and normalize the remaining local Beads/task git state in this repo after partial landing work completed on March 4, 2026.

Current known state as of 2026-03-04:
- Main checkout path: /Users/pnore/Documents/GitHub/codekiln/beans
- Main branch: main
- Main commit after cleanup work: 6eff8de
- origin/main already includes commit 6eff8de
- The only attached non-main worktree is the persistent metadata worktree at /Users/pnore/Documents/GitHub/codekiln/beans/.git/beads-worktrees/beads-sync on branch beads-sync
- beads-sync is the repo's Beads metadata branch and is not a normal feature/task branch to merge into main

What was already done on 2026-03-04:
- Loaded repo instructions from AGENTS.md
- Confirmed main was clean
- Confirmed beads-sync was the only attached task-related worktree
- Merged local branch codex/beans-1rz into main
- Pushed main to origin, resulting in main commit 6eff8de
- Deleted these safely ancestry-merged local branches with git branch -d:
  - codex/beans-1g2
  - codex/beans-1rz
  - codex/beans-7k3
  - codex/beans-swm

Branches/worktrees that still require follow-up:
- beads-sync (attached worktree)
- codex/beans-2wm (local branch, upstream exists at origin/codex/beans-2wm)
- codex/beans-7kp (local branch, no upstream shown)
- codex/beans-qqd-install-neovim (local branch, no upstream shown)

Important Beads issue context already checked:
- beans-e2z is CLOSED and corresponds to Beads daemon/direct-mode hardening work; beads-sync currently points at commit message "Sync Beads for beans-e2z"
- beans-1rz is CLOSED and was merged into main on 2026-03-04
- beans-2wm is CLOSED
- beans-7kp is CLOSED
- The remaining git branches are not necessarily safe to delete with plain git branch -d just because their Beads issues are CLOSED

Known analysis from 2026-03-04:
- git cherry -v main codex/beans-1rz showed one unapplied patch, which is why it was merged
- git merge-base --is-ancestor confirmed codex/beans-1g2, codex/beans-7k3, and codex/beans-swm were already merged into main before deletion
- codex/beans-2wm, codex/beans-7kp, and codex/beans-qqd-install-neovim are NOT ancestry-merged into main, so git branch -d would not be the right cleanup command unless their tips are first landed or you intentionally force-delete them
- Earlier symmetric-diff checks were misleading because main...branch reports branch-point-relative changes, not only tip-to-tip tree equality; re-check the actual tip trees before deciding

What the next agent should do:
1. Reconfirm the latest state with exact commands before making changes:
   - git status --short --branch
   - git worktree list
   - git branch -vv
   - bd --no-daemon list
   - bd --no-daemon show beans-2wm
   - bd --no-daemon show beans-7kp
2. Inspect each remaining branch relative to main using both ancestry and actual tree diff:
   - git merge-base --is-ancestor <branch> main
   - git log --oneline main..<branch>
   - git diff --stat main <branch>
   - git cherry -v main <branch>
3. Decide branch-by-branch whether the correct action is:
   - land it into main because it still contains desired work not in main
   - close/sync the related Beads metadata if the issue was not fully finished
   - remove the worktree if it is only leftover metadata/worktree state
   - delete the local branch safely with git branch -d if it becomes ancestry-merged
   - force-delete the local branch only if it is intentionally obsolete and the tip is confirmed not needed
4. For Beads-managed branches that still need a formal closeout, prefer the repo helpers described in AGENTS.md and docs/dev/beads.md:
   - use dev/beads-finish <issue-id> for close/sync when appropriate
   - use dev/land-the-plane <issue-id> from the task worktree when the task branch/worktree still exists in the expected form and should land into main
5. Be careful with beads-sync:
   - it is a persistent metadata branch/worktree, not a normal task branch
   - do not merge beads-sync into main
   - if cleanup includes deleting obsolete task worktrees, beads-sync should normally remain unless there is a very explicit repo-level reason to recreate it
6. If local/remote main has changed since 2026-03-04, use the repo's normal policy from AGENTS.md:
   - update local main from origin/main
   - merge the task branch into main locally
   - push main directly
7. After cleanup, re-run:
   - git worktree list
   - git branch -vv
   - bd --no-daemon ready
   - bd --no-daemon sync --check
   - bd --no-daemon sync --force (if metadata changes were made)

Open questions the next agent must resolve explicitly:
- Is codex/beans-2wm still needed as a distinct branch, or was its intent already superseded by later commits on main?
- Is codex/beans-7kp truly obsolete, or does its tip contain any content/config state that still needs to be preserved another way?
- Is codex/beans-qqd-install-neovim just a leftover local experiment/bean entry branch, or should that content be landed or archived elsewhere before deletion?
- Are there any stale remote branches that should also be cleaned up after local state is normalized?

Safety rules:
- Do not use destructive cleanup blindly
- Do not delete non-main branches solely because the associated Beads issue is CLOSED
- Verify branch tip content against current main before any forced delete
- Preserve the repo's main-first landing policy from AGENTS.md
acceptance_criteria:
- Remaining task/worktree branches are each either landed into main, intentionally preserved with rationale, or deleted with the correct git command
- No obsolete task worktrees remain attached after cleanup, except the persistent beads-sync metadata worktree if still required by repo policy
- main is synced with origin/main after any landing work
- Beads metadata is synced and verified when issue/workflow updates were made
- Final notes record what branches/worktrees were kept, landed, or deleted and why
notes:
Session evidence from 2026-03-04 cleanup pass:

Commands/results already observed:
- git worktree list showed:
  - /Users/pnore/Documents/GitHub/codekiln/beans on main
  - /Users/pnore/Documents/GitHub/codekiln/beans/.git/beads-worktrees/beads-sync on beads-sync
- git branch -vv initially showed:
  - beads-sync at 0f3e919 with upstream origin/beads-sync
  - codex/beans-1g2 at abc1492
  - codex/beans-1rz at 5fa4894 with upstream origin/codex/beans-1rz
  - codex/beans-2wm at 2f09a17 with upstream origin/codex/beans-2wm
  - codex/beans-7k3 at e790ee2 with upstream origin/codex/beans-7k3: gone
  - codex/beans-7kp at b64abcf
  - codex/beans-qqd-install-neovim at f17854b
  - codex/beans-swm at 70b3c61 with upstream origin/codex/beans-swm
- git log --left-right main...beads-sync showed beads-sync differs from main only as Beads metadata/history, not as a task branch to merge into main
- git diff --name-status main...beads-sync showed only .beads/issues.jsonl
- bd --no-daemon list showed open/ready issues unrelated to this cleanup and confirmed Beads was functioning in direct mode
- bd --no-daemon show confirmed these issues were already CLOSED:
  - beans-e2z
  - beans-1rz
  - beans-2wm
  - beans-7kp

Branch-specific checks already run:
- git cherry -v main codex/beans-1rz returned:
  + 5fa48945faed453ff143e32096123593437fef05 Add Dependabot and PR CI automation
  Conclusion: codex/beans-1rz still had one unapplied patch and should be landed.
- git cherry -v main codex/beans-2wm returned:
  - e955c8871af0829fce949cf25d4c8a24de7c6b5e Add strict land-the-plane workflow
  - 2f09a17dcbc861b4b96f37c7a5c6f3e4eee24981 Handle first-push land-the-plane branches
  Conclusion: patch-equivalent commits appear present on main, but branch tip is not ancestry-merged.
- git cherry -v main codex/beans-7kp returned:
  + cb6f703bd0c9df26c0811637a9184a4b587f9182 Fix optional bean attribute rendering and add regression check
  Conclusion at the time was ambiguous because other diff modes were misleading; re-check actual tip tree and commit containment before deleting.
- git cherry -v main codex/beans-qqd-install-neovim returned:
  - f17854b2d84de462d0ad3dbea2d9321953372caa bean entry 2026-02-16 mon cup1
  Conclusion: patch-equivalent content appears to exist on main, but branch tip is not ancestry-merged.
- git merge-base --is-ancestor confirmed these were already merged into main before deletion:
  - codex/beans-1g2
  - codex/beans-7k3
  - codex/beans-swm

Changes actually made on 2026-03-04:
- Ran:
  git fetch origin
  git pull --ff-only origin main
  git merge --no-edit codex/beans-1rz
  git push origin main
- Result: main advanced to commit 6eff8de
- Merge summary for codex/beans-1rz:
  - created .github/dependabot.yml
  - created .github/workflows/ci.yml
  - updated README.md
- Safely deleted with git branch -d after merge-status verification:
  - codex/beans-1g2
  - codex/beans-1rz
  - codex/beans-7k3
  - codex/beans-swm

State after that landing pass:
- git branch -vv showed remaining locals:
  - beads-sync at 0f3e919
  - codex/beans-2wm at 2f09a17
  - codex/beans-7kp at b64abcf
  - codex/beans-qqd-install-neovim at f17854b
  - main at 6eff8de
- git status --short --branch on main was clean
- git worktree list still showed only main and the beads-sync worktree

Caution from the session:
- main...branch symmetric diffs caused confusion because they are relative to the merge base and can make already-superseded branches look active.
- Before deleting codex/beans-2wm, codex/beans-7kp, or codex/beans-qqd-install-neovim, prefer exact checks against current main:
  - git diff --stat main <branch>
  - git log --oneline --decorate main..<branch>
  - git merge-base --is-ancestor <branch> main
  - git cherry -v main <branch>
- Do not merge beads-sync into main.

title: fix failed ci deploy to github pages starting with commit 1d2643d
notes:
Root cause: rss.xml.ts imported sanitize-html (missing dependency). Reworked RSS rendering to use @astrojs/markdown-remark + escapeHtml to avoid extra deps; build passes locally.
Closed: RSS no longer depends on sanitize-html; build passes locally.

title: Make Dependabot Astro 6 upgrade PR mergeable
description:
Follow up on Dependabot PR #22:
- https://github.com/codekiln/beans/pull/22
- Title: deps: bump the astro group across 1 directory with 3 updates

Current state:
- CI is failing on the PR.
- The grouped upgrade includes major bumps from astro 4.x to 6.0.4 and @astrojs/mdx 3.x to 5.0.0, plus a patch update to @astrojs/rss.
- Known Astro v6 upgrade areas called out in the PR body include Node 22 requirements, Vite 7 changes, and changed markdown heading ID generation.

Expected work:
1. Reproduce the CI failure locally on the PR branch or equivalent dependency set.
2. Identify and implement the repo changes needed for Astro 6 compatibility.
3. Verify with npm ci and npm run build, plus any targeted content/rendering checks that fail during investigation.
4. If the fixes are straightforward, push or otherwise prepare the PR for merge.
5. If the upgrade is too broad for one pass, leave a clear breakdown of blockers and the next required changes.
acceptance_criteria: Dependabot PR #22 is either updated to pass CI and become mergeable, or the task documents the specific incompatible code paths and required follow-up changes. Local verification should include npm ci and npm run build against the Astro 6 dependency set before merge.
notes: Landed Astro 6 content collection migration, validated with npm ci, npm run build, and npm run check:bean-rendering.

title: Include buddy comment in RSS with semantic demarcation
description: Expose buddy comment content in RSS item HTML so subscribers can see the virtual commentary without confusing it with primary author text.
acceptance_criteria:
- RSS generator includes buddy comment when present
- RSS markup includes clear semantic marker (for example figure/blockquote/cite + label) distinguishing virtual persona comment from entry body
- Comment output degrades safely in feed readers with limited CSS support
- Existing RSS content remains valid and unchanged for entries without buddy comments

title: Implement Rulesync skill/subagent to generate Rin Vale comment into bean frontmatter
description: Add the generation workflow that targets a bean slug/id, reads the current entry and relevant prior entries, and writes/replaces frontmatter buddy comment fields.
acceptance_criteria:
- Workflow can be invoked for a specific bean entry id/slug (for example 2026-03-05-bean1)
- Generated output updates YAML frontmatter only for comment metadata and tag inclusion
- Existing buddy comment is replaced on rerun
- Prompt/persona guidance yields Rin Vale voice with practical tasting analysis and brewing advice
- No patch-preview mode; direct file mutation

title: Add retro RPG CoffeeBuddyQuote Astro component and bean detail integration
description: Render frontmatter buddy comments at the bottom of bean entries using a distinct dialogue box style with static portrait on the right and persona nameplate.
acceptance_criteria:
- New Astro component renders avatar, name, title/subtitle, and comment body
- Bean detail template renders component after markdown body when comment data exists
- Visual design is clearly distinct from normal blockquote styling
- Core art direction is 16-bit retro RPG dialogue/portrait style; this takes priority over all other visual references
- Rin portrait may include a camp-style enamel mug as secondary inspiration (Smokey mug vibe), but artwork remains original and should not reduce retro readability/style fidelity
- Uses project path/base conventions for image assets and links
- Supports mobile and desktop layout
notes: Priority clarification (2026-03-05): Preserve 16-bit retro style first. Enamel mug reference is optional/secondary and must not interfere with the retro aesthetic.

title: Add Rin Vale virtual coffee buddy comments to bean entries
description: Introduce an opt-in AI-generated coffee buddy comment for bean entries. Comment data is stored in bean frontmatter and rendered at the bottom of entry content with a distinct retro-RPG dialogue visual treatment.
acceptance_criteria:
- Bean schema supports optional buddy comment metadata in frontmatter
- Persona is fixed as Rin Vale with title Campfire Cupping Companion, but editable in config/prompt
- Command/skill replaces existing comment for a target bean entry (no append)
- Tagged posts include a consistent label for buddy-commented entries
- RSS output includes the comment with semantic demarcation from author-written body
- Detail-page rendering is visually distinct from existing blockquotes

title: Harden Beads daemon and startup mode for local/worktree sessions
description: Investigate and fix unreliable Beads daemon behavior in this repo so common startup commands do not hang in local or worktree sessions. Observations from 2026-03-01: bd show beans-1rz hung until rerun with --no-daemon; dev/beads-start also stalled because it shells through bd commands without forcing direct mode; .git write failures were separate sandbox issues, but the daemon/direct-storage behavior itself appeared flaky independent of that. Scope: inspect .beads daemon setup, hooks, socket/lock behavior, and repo docs; decide whether repo helpers should always force --no-daemon or whether daemon configuration can be made reliable; document the chosen policy and update startup helpers accordingly.
acceptance_criteria:
- Root cause of the hanging daemon-path behavior is documented
- Repo startup helpers use the reliable mode by default
- Docs explain when to use daemon vs --no-daemon in this repo
- Starting work on a bead does not hang in typical local/worktree sessions
notes: Reproduced on 2026-03-01: beans-e2z existed in .beads/beads.db and via bd show, but was absent from both the main checkout .beads/issues.jsonl and the beads-sync worktree JSONL. Plain bd commands hung intermittently until rerun with --no-daemon. A stale daemon then re-imported from beads-sync in a loop and reverted exports. Repo helpers/docs now force direct mode; durable export should be verified against .git/beads-worktrees/beads-sync/.beads/issues.jsonl.

title: Add terms command namespace and glossary pages
description: Implement Beans term namespace with index and detail routes, add terms content collection/schema, and seed initial RDT glossary entry.
notes:
Implemented in commit d051adf; tracking task added after code delivery to align Beads planning records.
Clarification: RDT is the sample first term seed only; this task tracks the reusable terms namespace and glossary framework for additional entries.

title: Document source-of-truth workflow for Beads and code across environments
description:
Document a single, explicit workflow so AI agents and humans consistently handle task metadata vs code across all execution contexts (Codespaces, local devcontainer, Codex Desktop on work/home machines, and Codex Cloud).

Scope:
- Define source of truth for Beads metadata and product code.
- Explain expected branch/worktree behavior (including beads-sync usage).
- Add concise environment-agnostic startup/preflight instructions.
- Add clear do/don't examples for committing and syncing issue metadata.
acceptance_criteria:
- AGENTS.md clearly states Beads metadata source of truth and code source of truth.
- README.md (or docs/dev/beads.md) includes a short operator-focused summary.
- Instructions include at least one concrete command sequence for starting work and syncing metadata.
- Guidance is explicit for all listed environments and does not conflict with existing Beads docs.
- A new contributor/agent can determine the correct commit target (working branch vs beads-sync) without ambiguity.
notes:
Implementation plan:
1. Audit existing guidance in AGENTS.md and docs/dev/beads.md for ambiguity.
2. Write a minimal canonical policy section (source-of-truth + commit targets).
3. Add a short preflight checklist and command examples.
4. Cross-check wording against current Beads behavior and update references.

title: Implement Questions content collection and schema
description:
Implement a top-level Questions content collection with validated schema.

Scope:
- Add collection definition in Astro content config for questions.
- Add required date-created property for questions.
- Add minimal metadata needed to support homepage listing and command rendering.
- Add starter example question entry following project tone and conventions.
acceptance_criteria:
- Questions collection compiles and validates through Astro content schema.
- Questions entries require date-created metadata in a consistent format.
- Sample questions content entry exists.
- Generated routes use /beans/ base-prefixed URLs for preview/testing.
- Documentation/comments are sufficient for future entries.
notes: Refactored scope: Terms is complete and no longer part of this implementation issue; this issue tracks Questions-only work.

title: Requirements intake for Questions content space
description:
Run a focused requirements interview to define the top-level Questions content space in Beans.

Scope:
- Confirm information architecture for Questions content and how it differs from existing Beans content types.
- Define minimum metadata, including required date-created semantics for questions.
- Define CLI-style command patterns that surface an individual question.
- Confirm how Questions should appear on the front page reverse-chron timeline and tie-breaking rules.
acceptance_criteria:
- Interview transcript captured in issue notes.
- Decision summary includes required fields for Questions.
- Decision summary includes slug strategy and canonical URL patterns under /beans/.
- Decision summary includes CLI command format examples for Questions.
- Implementation-ready checklist produced for follow-on tasks.
notes: Refactored scope: Terms is complete and tracked outside this issue; this issue now covers Questions-only requirements.

title: Integrate Questions into homepage reverse-chron stream
description:
Update the Beans front page to display Questions in reverse-chron positions, including the simulated CLI command that would retrieve each item.

Scope:
- Merge Questions into existing homepage listing flow without breaking current sections.
- Render each question item in CLI-inspired style consistent with Beans.
- Preserve ordering guarantees by created date with deterministic tie-breaking.
acceptance_criteria:
- Homepage includes Questions items in reverse chronological order.
- Each rendered question includes an appropriate simulated CLI command.
- Existing homepage content rendering remains intact.
- Manual preview confirms /beans/ prefixed routes and expected ordering.
notes: Refactored scope: Terms work is complete and removed from this issue; this issue now tracks Questions-only homepage integration.

title: Migrate Codex memories into Rulesync and set Rulesync as AI config system of record
description: Import all existing guidance from .codex/memories into .rulesync/rules using rulesync import workflows, reconcile overlaps, and ensure generated downstream AI config files preserve current behavior. Add explicit project guidance that Rulesync is the system of record for AI coding configuration, analogous to Beads for task tracking.
acceptance_criteria: 1) All active .codex/memories guidance is represented in .rulesync/rules with no loss of critical instructions. 2) AGENTS and generated tool-specific outputs clearly state Rulesync as the AI config system of record. 3) Document the migration/update workflow for future rule changes (edit rulesync, then generate). 4) Validate generation output and confirm no accidental rule regressions.

title: Use Dependabot at CI level to keep dependencies updated
description: Add Dependabot automation for GitHub Actions and npm dependencies so updates are proposed continuously and validated in CI before merge.
acceptance_criteria:
- Dependabot config exists under .github/dependabot.yml and checks npm + GitHub Actions on a recurring schedule
- CI runs for Dependabot PRs with the same required checks as other PRs
- Auto-generated PRs include sensible grouping/rate limits to avoid noisy update spam
- Docs briefly explain how Dependabot is configured and how to tune cadence
notes: Added weekly Dependabot updates for npm and GitHub Actions, added PR CI for build plus bean rendering, and documented tuning points in README.

title: Review and land open Dependabot PRs
description:
Review the currently open Dependabot pull requests for this repository and land the safe updates.

Open PRs to evaluate:
- #22 deps: bump the astro group across 1 directory with 3 updates
- #19 deps: bump the development-tooling group with 2 updates
- #17 ci: bump the github-actions group with 3 updates

Expected follow-up flow:
1. Inspect each PR's diff, status checks, and release notes/changelog impact.
2. Merge any PR that is low-risk and passing.
3. If a PR is risky or failing, leave notes about why it should not be merged yet.
4. Pull local main after any merges so the local checkout matches GitHub.
acceptance_criteria: Open Dependabot PRs #22, #19, and #17 are reviewed; safe PRs are merged; local main is pulled/rebased to include any merged changes; follow-up notes capture anything deferred or blocked.
notes:
Started PR review for Dependabot PRs #22, #19, and #17. #22 is currently failing CI; validating #19 locally before merging and #17 appears mergeable based on passing CI and workflow-only changes.
Reviewed Dependabot PRs #22, #19, and #17. Merged #17 (GitHub Actions updates) and #19 (development-tooling updates) after confirming passing CI; additionally validated #19 locally with npm ci, npm run build, and npx rulesync generate in a temporary worktree. Pulled local main to 9341ff91315d5386c224ecddcf0989b745714de6 after the merges. Left #22 open because its CI is failing and it includes major Astro upgrades that need a separate compatibility pass.
close_reason: Reviewed open Dependabot PRs, merged safe updates (#17 and #19), pulled local main, and documented that #22 remains open pending Astro 6 compatibility work.

title: Create bean log 2026-03-16 mon 0530
Enable MDX bean entries for inline authored components
description: Add Astro MDX support and allow bean entries that need inline rich components to use .mdx while preserving existing .md bean posts.
acceptance_criteria:
- Beans build successfully with mixed .md and .mdx entries.
- A bean entry can author an inline companion comment component in MDX.
- Existing non-MDX bean entries continue rendering unchanged.
notes:
Design iteration (session summary):

1) Started with Rin-style Coffee Buddy Quote treatment: portrait, "Companion comment" kicker, dialogue box with layered borders. Felt too much like a separate card.

2) Simplified: removed "Companion comment" and name-above-quote; reduced borders (single outer + single portrait border); moved portrait to the right; aligned top of quote with top of portrait. Still felt like a card.

3) Tried full 16-bit JRPG dialogue panel: SNES palette (dark blue #1c1c4c), 2–3px layered pixel borders, name at top of box, portrait attached right. Feedback: read as HUD/status panel, not spoken dialogue; too much empty blue; name at top-left like a window title.

4) Pivoted to terminal-native quoted aside with three variants: (a) terminal = box-drawing border, quote left, avatar + name right; (b) bubble = rounded speech bubble, circular avatar, name below avatar; (c) ascii = quote in ASCII comment box, avatar in ASCII frame, caption below. None confirmed as final; we have not landed on a design that works yet.

Profile image linking: The companion portrait (avatar) is wrapped in a link to the companion detail page (getCompanionPath(slug)). The companion name below the avatar also links to the same detail page. Both use semantic <a href={companionHref}> in the template. Portrait link is not aria-hidden so it’s available to assistive tech.

---

NEXT STEP — Refactor the companion quote component (for a new agent):

Goal: Create a clean quote bubble that feels like a simple Slack-style message but adapted for a terminal-style site.

Structure: Use a semantic blockquote. Inside the blockquote create two visual zones:
1. Quote text (left side)
2. Companion attribution panel (right side)

Layout requirements:
• The quote text should appear on the left side of the blockquote.
• The companion panel should appear inside the blockquote on the right side.
• The companion panel must hug the right edge of the quote box.
• The companion panel should contain: a circular profile image; the companion name below the image.
• The profile image should link to the companion detail page.
• The name should also link to the companion detail page.

Visual hierarchy: Quote text should be visually dominant. The companion panel should be smaller and visually secondary. The avatar should be small and circular. The name should appear directly under the avatar and centered.

Behavior: The quote box should size naturally to its content. Do not stretch it to full page width. Keep the layout compact.

Aesthetic goals: simple; quiet; slightly reminiscent of Slack message bubbles; compatible with the site's terminal aesthetic; avoid heavy UI chrome or dashboard-like panels.

Avoid: full-width quote panels; placing the avatar outside the quote box; placing the name above the quote; large portrait blocks that dominate the layout; dark saturated background colors.

The result should read immediately as: "this character said this line."
---
ASCII variant WIP (2026-03-15): Implemented but still visually incomprehensible.

Current state:
- CompanionComment.astro has a dedicated ascii branch: blockquote contains .companion-comment__ascii-box with border rows (+ / stretchy line / + / +---+) and a content row (| body cell | portrait cell |). Comment markers /* and */ are on separate lines above/below the quote; body has ::before \" * \" prefix.
- In the browser the ascii box still renders as fragmented: +, -, and | characters are scattered and do not form a coherent border; /* and */ do not clearly frame the quote text; the profile image still appears small and square for some users/viewports, not larger and rectangular as specified.

Layout fixes already tried: .companion-comment--ascii .companion-comment__quote { display: block }; .companion-comment__ascii-box { min-width: 18rem; max-width: 100% }; flex-wrap: nowrap on border and content rows; min-width on body cell and .companion-comment__ascii-h. The box still does not render as a readable terminal-style comment block.

PROMPT FOR NEXT AGENT — Continue the ascii variant of CompanionComment:

1. Open src/components/CompanionComment.astro and focus only on the ascii variant (leave terminal and bubble unchanged).
2. Fix the layout so the ASCII box renders as a single coherent comment block: top border (e.g. +----+---+), left edge |, comment lines (/*, * quote, */), right edge with portrait frame (| +---+ | img | +---+ |), bottom border. Ensure + - | characters align and do not wrap or fragment.
3. Use a larger rectangular profile image for the ascii variant only (e.g. 56x72px or similar, border-radius: 0). Verify in the browser that the portrait is visibly rectangular and larger than the small circular avatars in terminal/bubble.
4. Ensure /* and */ sit on their own lines and clearly bracket the quote text with no overlap or visual clash with the border.
5. Test on the March 14 bean page (e.g. /beans/log/2026-03-14-0820/) in the taste section: the \"nuttiness\" companion comment uses variant=\"ascii\" and should read as a clear terminal-style comment block with rectangular portrait.

title: Fix Beads closeout workflow contradictions and clarify tracked metadata paths
description:
During beans-zpt.1 closeout, the repo's Beads workflow contradicted itself in several places: the task worktree carried an invalid .beads redirect warning, dev/land-the-plane failed on untracked files that were legitimate task output, Beads metadata updates dirtied the root main checkout after dev/beads-finish, and the prune helper did not remove a closed task worktree. The repo guidance also uses imprecise terms like durable/non-durable when what matters operationally is which metadata file is the authoritative tracked export and which files are only local working state.

Fix the helpers, checks, and docs so a normal Beads task can be started, closed, landed, synced, and pruned without manual repair steps. Make the language explicit about which metadata file is authoritative, which files are just local checkout state, and which branch/worktree must contain the committed export.
acceptance_criteria:
- dev/beads-start, dev/beads-finish, dev/land-the-plane, and dev/beads-prune-closed-worktrees are audited against the actual repo worktree layout and updated where needed
- The closeout path no longer dirties root main in a way that blocks landing, or land-the-plane intentionally tolerates/cleans that state
- Worktree redirect wiring is verified so task worktrees do not emit misleading missing-.beads warnings
- The prune helper reliably removes closed worktrees like worktrees/beans-<issue-id>
- Docs replace vague durable/non-durable wording with explicit statements about authoritative tracked export files, local working files, and required commits/pushes
- Smoke checks cover the failure modes seen during beans-zpt.1 closeout

title: Add list subcommand routes and redirect collection roots
description: Add explicit list routes for collection-style entities so the simulated CLI commands and the URL structure have parity. For example, if the page chrome says 'bean companion list', the collection entry route should live at /beans/companion/list/ and /beans/companion/ should redirect there.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add list routes for collection index pages where the UI presents a CLI-style list command
- Redirect collection roots such as /beans/companion/ to their /beans/<entity>/list/ counterpart when appropriate
- Keep detail routes and internal navigation working after the redirect/list split
- Verify the simulated CLI command text matches the routed URL structure

title: Normalize entity collection routes to singular nouns
description: Audit Beans entity collection URLs and align collection/index routes to a singular naming scheme so the information architecture matches the CLI tone more consistently. The current site mixes plural collection roots like /questions/ and /roasters/ with singular roots like /companion/, which makes routing and command naming feel inconsistent.
acceptance_criteria:
- Decide and document the singular route convention for entity collection roots across Beans
- Update the affected pages, helpers, and internal links to use the chosen singular route names consistently
- Preserve working redirects from legacy plural URLs so existing links do not break
- Verify the resulting URL structure still uses the /beans/ base prefix in previews and links

title: Add /scan-for-questions Rulesync command for bean entries
description:
Add a Rulesync command `/scan-for-questions <bean-entry-post-reference-or-slug>` that scans a bean entry for coffee-related questions, files each detected question into its own question page, and replaces the original in-post question with a filed-question link.

The command should accept either a bean entry slug or a full post reference such as `/beans/log/2026-03-10-bean1/`.

For each detected coffee-related question:
- create a dedicated question page under the Beans questions area
- include the question text on that page
- include a backreference to the originating bean post
- replace the original question in the source post with `Filed [<question text>](<question page link>).`

Example target behavior:
- input: `/scan-for-questions /beans/log/2026-03-10-bean1/`
- detect the question about when to clean the Timemore grinder
- create a question page like `beans/questions/when-to-clean-grinder/`
- link the source post to that dedicated question page
acceptance_criteria:
- The command accepts either a bean post reference or bean slug.
- Coffee-related questions in the target post are identified and extracted.
- Each extracted question gets a dedicated question page with the question text and a backreference to the originating bean post.
- The original question in the bean post is replaced with `Filed [<question text>](<question page link>).`
- Running the command on `/beans/log/2026-03-10-bean1/` files the Timemore grinder cleaning question into a dedicated question page and updates the source post accordingly.

title: Simplify bean log identifiers for single-entry days
description:
Research summary:
- Current bean log display text and navigation hard-code `beanKey` across the homepage stream, detail page prompt/title/nav, companion logbook, coffee detail references, and the bean-rendering check.
- Bean content filenames and frontmatter IDs currently use `<date>-<beanKey>`; Astro slugs therefore expose `-bean1` even when the day has only one entry.
- Existing multi-cup days all have a top-level `time` value, so disambiguation can prefer 24-hour time when there is more than one entry on the same date, with numeric fallback reserved for missing-time cases.
- The current command template in the bean-entry guidance still requires `$ bean log <YYYY-MM-DD> <beanKey>`, so docs/content conventions will need a follow-up update when implementation begins.

Implementation intent:
- Omit the trailing identifier from default bean log commands and labels when a date has exactly one bean entry.
- When a date has multiple bean entries, prefer a military-time disambiguator derived from the entry time; if time is unavailable, fall back to a simple ordinal such as `1` or `2`.
- Preserve stable routing for existing entries or define a redirect/back-compat plan before changing public slugs.
design: Likely implementation starts in shared bean data helpers by grouping beans per date and deriving a display/route identifier from collision count plus `time`. The issue should avoid leading with a content migration until a route-compat strategy is chosen.
acceptance_criteria:
- Document the chosen identifier rules for command text, labels, and URLs before coding.
- Identify and update all affected rendering surfaces: homepage stream, bean detail page prompt/title/nav, companion references, coffee detail references, and relevant checks/tests.
- Decide whether existing content files/frontmatter keep `beanKey` internally, migrate to a new field, or derive identifiers per date at build time.
- If public slugs change, include backward-compatibility handling for existing `/beans/log/<date>-beanN/` links.

title: Overhaul header navigation into a mobile-friendly command menu
description:
The current header navigation wraps awkwardly as the command list grows. Redesign the site navigation so the header works cleanly across viewport sizes, with a mobile-friendly menu pattern that preserves the CLI-inspired Beans tone.

User intent for this epic:
- Replace the clunky wrapping header nav with a cleaner responsive navigation system
- Use the simulated prompt `🫘>` as the primary header/menu trigger instead of a generic hamburger icon
- Move the light/dark theme switch into the menu rather than leaving it exposed in the main header row
- Keep the command-oriented visual language and avoid losing discoverability on desktop
acceptance_criteria:
- Header navigation no longer wraps awkwardly at common viewport widths
- Mobile navigation uses the `🫘>` prompt motif as the primary menu trigger
- Theme switching is available inside the menu
- The final solution remains consistent with the site's CLI-inspired aesthetic and works across desktop and mobile

title: Investigate current header/navigation implementation and failure modes
description: Audit the current navigation/header implementation to document why the command links wrap awkwardly and where the current layout becomes clunky. Capture the relevant components, styles, breakpoints, and theme-toggle placement constraints.
acceptance_criteria:
- Current header/nav implementation paths are identified
- Awkward wrap/failure cases are documented with viewport assumptions
- Constraints affecting menu placement, prompt icon use, and theme-toggle relocation are recorded

title: Propose creative responsive navigation directions for Beans
description: Develop design directions for a responsive Beans navigation that stays intentional rather than generic. Explore how the `🫘>` prompt motif can act as the menu trigger, how desktop and mobile states should differ, and how the light/dark switch should live inside the menu.
acceptance_criteria:
- At least one strong recommended navigation direction is described
- The proposal covers desktop behavior, mobile behavior, and menu interaction
- The proposal explicitly includes the `🫘>` prompt trigger and in-menu theme switch
- The proposal keeps the CLI-inspired Beans voice intact

title: Implement responsive header command menu with prompt trigger and in-menu theme toggle
description: Implement the approved header/navigation redesign. Replace the current wrapping link row with a responsive command menu pattern, use the simulated prompt `🫘>` as the menu trigger, and move the light/dark toggle into the menu while keeping navigation usable on larger screens too.
acceptance_criteria:
- Header/menu implementation matches the approved responsive direction
- The `🫘>` prompt is used as the main header/menu trigger instead of a hamburger icon
- Navigation is usable on mobile and desktop without awkward wrapping
- Theme toggle is moved inside the menu
- Relevant previews or verification notes cover the updated header behavior

title: add bean entry for 2026-03-10 cup1
Update Beads helper scripts for current bd CLI
description: The repo's Beads helper scripts have drifted from the installed bd CLI. Current breakages observed during beans-fji closeout: git commit and dev/beads-start hit an unsupported 'bd hook' subcommand via the repo's hook path, and dev/beads-finish/dev/land-the-plane still call 'bd sync --force', which is no longer supported by the installed bd version. Update the helper scripts and any related checks/docs so the repo's standard Beads startup and landing flows work end-to-end again without manual workarounds like --no-verify, direct bd sync commands, or helper bypasses.
acceptance_criteria:
- Starting a task with dev/beads-start no longer fails because of an unsupported bd hook path
- Committing in the repo no longer requires --no-verify to bypass broken Beads hook integration
- dev/beads-finish and dev/land-the-plane work with the installed bd CLI and no longer call unsupported flags
- Related smoke checks/docs/rules are updated to match the supported bd workflow
- A Beads-managed task can be started, committed, finished, and landed without the manual compatibility workarounds used during beans-fji closeout
notes: Resolved in codex/beans-jqm while landing beans-jqm: dev/beads-start now refreshes shared git hooks with 'bd hooks install --force'; dev/beads-finish now uses supported bd 0.42.0 flags ('--notes' plus explicit export to .git/beads-worktrees/beads-sync/.beads/issues.jsonl); dev/land-the-plane no longer calls unsupported 'bd sync --force'; smoke checks/docs/rules were updated and npm run check:beads-start, check:beads-finish, check:land-the-plane, and build all passed.

title: Default land-the-plane checks and follow-up behavior
description: Adjust dev/land-the-plane so it has sensible automation-friendly defaults for validation and follow-up handling, while still allowing explicit one-off overrides from the human operator.
notes: Made land-the-plane default to repo validation and no follow-up issue unless explicitly overridden.

title: Add land-the-plane session closeout guidance and helper
description: Create a first-class repo-level land-the-plane workflow that builds on the existing Beads finish path. Add documentation for the end-of-session checklist, include cleanup/handoff guidance, and provide a helper command that standardizes the sequence for Beans work.
notes:
Added land-the-plane helper, documentation, and smoke coverage.
Tightened the land-the-plane flow to require checks, follow-up accounting, rebase, push, and origin sync verification.
Tightened the land-the-plane flow to require checks, follow-up accounting, rebase, push, and origin sync verification.

title: Add editorial title triage skill for weak bean entry titles
description: Create a Rulesync-backed AI skill that reviews bean entry titles, identifies the 10 least interesting titles in the archive, and then updates those posts by selecting a more compelling quoted phrase from each post body. The target sensibility is the kind of juicy, high-signal pull-quote an editor at The Economist, Harper's Bazaar, The New Yorker, or The New York Times might appreciate: specific, vivid, and earned by the text rather than clickbait. Replacement titles must be all lowercase and no more than five words. Use the March 2, 2026 bean post (`2026-03-02-bean1`) as the style reference for title form. The workflow should make the selection process reproducible enough that future agents can rerun it on demand and then apply the resulting title edits to the chosen entries.
acceptance_criteria:
- A local Rulesync-managed skill exists that tells Codex when and how to identify the 10 weakest or least interesting bean entry titles
- The skill defines a practical rubric for weak-title detection and for choosing stronger replacement quotes from the post body
- Replacement titles are derived from the post text, are all lowercase, and are no more than five words
- The workflow uses the March 2, 2026 bean post (`2026-03-02-bean1`) as the reference for title form
- The workflow includes updating the selected bean entries with improved titles derived from their own text
- The quoted replacements preserve the voice of the posts and avoid fabricated wording
- Any generated agent outputs are refreshed with `npx rulesync generate` if the source of truth lives under `.rulesync/`
- Content validation/build passes after the title updates

title: Add person tags to bean posts and backfill the archive
description: Start tagging people mentioned in bean posts with a canonical tag format like `Person/<FirstName> <LastName>`. Apply this both prospectively and retroactively across the existing archive. Include musicians being listened to, composers/performers referenced in embeds or prose, and other explicitly referenced people mentioned in the posts. As part of the work, define how person tags should coexist with the existing listening/topic tag patterns and normalize casing/spacing expectations for the new tag family.
acceptance_criteria:
- A canonical person-tag format is defined and documented through implementation, using `Person/<FirstName> <LastName>` or an explicitly approved variant if constraints require adjustment
- Existing bean posts are reviewed and backfilled with person tags where named people are referenced
- Listening-related people such as musicians, composers, and performers are included when they are named in the post
- Non-music referenced people named in prose are also tagged when they are clearly identifiable
- The approach avoids ambiguous tags for partially identified people or explains the fallback rule for those cases
- Content validation/build passes after the tag backfill

title: Add attribute history script and Rulesync skill for content forensics
description: Add a repo script that reports the history of a frontmatter attribute over time, similar to inspecting recent values of fields like `brew.method` across `src/content/beans/*.md`. The script should be usable from the terminal and a local Codex skill should guide agents to use it for questions about how an attribute has changed over time. Because this repo treats generated agent files as derived output, the durable source of truth should live under `.rulesync/` where appropriate, with any generated outputs refreshed via `npx rulesync generate`.
acceptance_criteria:
- A script exists in the repo that can print attribute values over time for content entries (for example, recent values for a frontmatter path)
- The script documentation or interface makes clear which content set/path formats are supported
- A local skill exists that tells Codex when and how to use the script for attribute-history questions
- If the skill or guidance is sourced from Rulesync-managed files, the generated agent outputs are refreshed with `npx rulesync generate`
- The workflow is documented enough that future agents do not need to rediscover it ad hoc

title: Remove redundant brew.method from bean entries
description: `brew.method` currently duplicates the purpose of the linked `brew.recipe` in bean frontmatter. Remove the field from the bean content schema and from existing content, keeping the recipe link as the canonical structured way to describe brew approach. Audit any code paths that still expect `brew.method` and remove or migrate them.
acceptance_criteria:
- `src/content/config.ts` no longer includes `brew.method` in the beans schema
- Existing bean entries no longer store `brew.method` in frontmatter
- No component, page, or script reads `bean.data.brew.method`
- Content validation/build passes after the migration
notes:
Started in worktree codex/beans-7k3. Removed brew.method from beans schema and migrated existing bean frontmatter off the field. Verified no remaining references with rg; npm run build and npm run check:bean-rendering both pass in the worktree.
Removed redundant brew.method from the bean schema and migrated existing bean entries to rely on brew.recipe.

title: Add verification for list-only homepage and detail navigation
description: Verify /beans/ shows list-only entries and no embedded post body content, with working links to /beans/log/<slug> detail pages. Include preview screenshot for Codex and note expected 429 risk reduction from fewer embeds on index.

title: Switch homepage to bean log list-only index
description: Main /beans/ page should render as a lightweight list of entries (no full post bodies), following terms-page prompt style with 'bean log list'. Each item links to /beans/log/<slug>/ for full detail. Goal: reduce embedded Spotify iframes/content load to avoid rate-limit 429 failures.

title: Add :wq insignia at end of every bean post
description: Add a small monospace :wq insignia at the end of each bean post as a visual signature. The marker should reference Vim save-and-quit while also reading like a QED-style closing mark, and it should fit the existing CLI-inspired tone without distracting from post content.
acceptance_criteria:
- Every bean post detail page renders a trailing :wq insignia at the end of the post content
- Insignia is styled in monospace and visually subtle
- Placement reads as a closing signature (QED-like) rather than part of body copy
- List/archive views are not negatively affected
- Existing /beans/ base-path handling remains correct

title: Import Parable Guatemala ACODIHUE Heuheutenango Organic coffee entry and wire recent-post backlink
description:
Create a new coffee entry under Parable Coffee Co. for https://www.parablecoffeeco.com/product-page/guatemala-organic using the existing roaster entry style (see finca-los-angeles.md, mocha-java.md, sojourner-blend.md).

Research captured from source payload:
- Product name: Guatemala ACODIHUE Heuheutenango Organic
- Cup notes: Cocoa, apple butter, juicy sweetness
- Roast: Medium
- Producer/Farm: ACODIHUE Assoc., various smallholders
- Variety: Bourbon, Catimor, Caturra, Maragogype, Pache
- Altitude: 1400-2200 masl
- Region: Huehuetenango
- Process: Washed
- Narrative includes ACODIHUE coop context and fair-trade/organic positioning

Also audit the most recent bean log post and ensure it references the new internal coffee page where contextually appropriate (internal /beans/coffees/... link, not only external product URL). The current newest bean post file is src/content/beans/2026-02-22-bean1.md; verify whether it should reference this Guatemala coffee or another recent post should carry the backlink.
acceptance_criteria:
1) New markdown file is added at src/content/roasters/parable-coffee-co/<slug>.md and passes the roasters collection schema.
2) Entry includes source link to the product page and captures cup profile, roast level, origin facts (producer, variety, altitude, region, process), plus concise narrative notes in markdown body.
3) Slug/path naming is consistent with existing Parable coffee entries and site routing.
4) Latest relevant bean post contains an internal link to /beans/coffees/parable-coffee-co/<slug>/ (or is intentionally left unchanged with rationale documented in bead notes if not applicable).
5) Project checks for content/build pass for touched files.
close_reason: Imported Guatemala ACODIHUE coffee entry, added recent-post backlink, and fixed bean-rendering check

title: Analyze and fix .gitignore
description: Review local .gitignore modifications, determine intended ignore behavior, and apply a safe fix without reverting unrelated user work.

title: Fix check:bean-rendering date-header assertion
description: The bean rendering check fails after adding 2026-02-22-bean2 with 'Bean detail page is missing the expected date-only header'. Update the check so it validates stable structure without brittle assumptions.

title: Make h3 visually distinct from h4 with clear hierarchy
description:
Adjust markdown content heading styles so h3 and h4 are clearly distinguishable and convey hierarchy.

Context: Prior visual-distinction tasks include beans-2xn (inline code/backticks) and beans-2xl (blockquote styling). Reuse the same approach: improve distinction while preserving readability and contrast.

Idea to evaluate: render heading markers as a presentational cue, e.g. h3 appears with a prefixed '### ' token and h4 with '#### ' token (visual-only; semantic heading levels must remain h3/h4 in the HTML).

Acceptance criteria:
- h3 and h4 are visually distinct in bean markdown content.
- There is a clear visual hierarchy between h3 and h4 (size, weight, spacing, or marker treatment).
- Contrast/readability remain acceptable.
- Existing CLI-inspired tone is preserved.
- Add/adjust preview evidence for a /beans/ route showing both heading levels.
close_reason: Implemented h3/h4 hierarchy styling, added CLI marker treatment, and captured /beans/ preview evidence

title: Simplify bean entry title formatting and add time prompt header
description: Refactor bean entry rendering to avoid repeating title text, convert the timestamp/header line into a CLI-like prompt linked to the detail page, and remove decorative separator text from title section. Preserve h2 semantics and existing CLI tone.

title: Honor optional bean attributes in rendering; avoid undefined time
description: Align bean entry rendering with project spirit: treat all attributes as optional and do not display placeholders or labels for missing values. Specifically ensure bean log headers do not show `undefined` when `time` is absent.
acceptance_criteria:
- Rendering logic only shows attributes when values are present (no empty/undefined placeholders)

title: Enable AI screenshot inspection via Playwright MCP
description: Provide a container-local vision loop so AI agents can capture screenshots for UI verification (Codex → MCP → headless browser → screenshot → model). Set up Playwright MCP server, ensure Chromium install, and document/automate devcontainer startup. Include guidance for using container-local http://localhost and screenshots/fullPage.
notes: Duplicate of beans-2xo.

title: Make inline code/backtick styling more distinct
description: Adjust inline code/backtick styling to be more visually distinct while preserving contrast ratios. Relate this work to the blockquote styling task so both are reviewed together.

title: Install and configure Playwright skill for Codex previews
description: Add setup steps for installing Playwright and configuring it as a Codex skill or MCP integration to enable UI previews and screenshots from the running dev server. Provide a container-local vision loop (Codex → MCP → headless browser → screenshot → model). Ensure Chromium install, and document/automate devcontainer startup (e.g., postCreateCommand/postStartCommand). Include guidance for using container-local http://localhost and fullPage screenshots.
notes: Learned: Codex needs a container-local browser (Playwright MCP) and a stable MCP URL (http://localhost:5173/mcp) with npx -y to avoid prompts. Also, @playwright/mcp uses --viewport-size, not --viewport.

title: Configure Codex to skip permissions by default in Codespaces
description: Configure Codex defaults so that when running inside GitHub Codespaces, permission prompts are skipped automatically (use Codespaces environment variables to detect the environment). This should be repo-local and not affect non-Codespaces runs.
acceptance_criteria:
- Codex uses skip-permissions by default when Codespaces env vars are present
- Detection relies on GitHub Codespaces environment variables (not a hardcoded path)
- Non-Codespaces runs keep existing permission behavior
- Document the setting location and env var check
notes: Implemented repo-local Codespaces Codex defaults: added .codex codespaces profile, devcontainer setup-codex shell wrapper keyed on CODESPACES/CODESPACE_NAME, and docs/dev/codex.md.

title: Enable reliable UI previews for bean posts in Codex

Example desired preview: /beans/log/2026-02-05-bean1/ on port 4321.

notes: Learned: Blockquote styles only apply when markdown content carries the .markdown class; the bean log needed .bean-content.markdown. Also needed real italic font variants (Source Serif 4) to make quotes visibly distinct.

title: Auto-start/attach tmux in Codespaces terminals
description: In GitHub Codespaces, tmux server dies when the container stops. Add an auto-start/auto-attach hook so new terminals attach to the last tmux session (or create one) during interactive shells, without nesting inside tmux.
acceptance_criteria: - Opening a new Codespaces terminal attaches to existing tmux session if present\n- If no session exists, a new session is created automatically\n- No nested tmux when already inside a session
notes: Added Codespaces auto-attach snippet to ~/.bashrc and ~/.zshrc in .devcontainer/setup-tmux.sh; updated docs/dev/tmux.md with behavior.
